subject: What 3 Research Papers Tell Us About BIP Failures
previewText: The surprising truth about why most Behavior Intervention Plans fail.
body:
Hi [Name],

We've all been there. You spend hours writing what you think is the perfect Behavior Intervention Plan, only to see it fall flat. The behavior doesn't improve, the teacher is frustrated, and you're back to the drawing board.

Why does this happen so often? The research actually gives us some clear answers.

I've reviewed the literature and distilled the findings into a simple, easy-to-read summary of the top 3 reasons why most BIPs fail. In this short article, you'll learn:

*   The most common reason why BIPs are ineffective (it's not what you think).
*   How to ensure that your interventions are a good 'contextual fit' for the classroom.
*   A simple strategy to increase treatment integrity and get better results.

If you're tired of writing BIPs that don't work, this article is a must-read.

[Link to the Article]

Best,

The BehaviorSchool Team
